name: SystemPrompt MCP Server
what_it_does: SystemPrompt MCP server enabling AI agents to manage and work with system prompts — creating, storing, and retrieving system prompts, validating prompt safety, managing prompt templates, and integrating prompt management capabilities into agent-driven AI application development and prompt engineering workflows.
use_cases: Managing a library of system prompts from AI application agents
Retrieving appropriate system prompts for different agent contexts
Validating and testing prompt templates from prompt engineering agents
Building prompt versioning and management systems from DevOps agents
Organizing prompts for multi-agent systems from orchestration agents
Auditing and reviewing system prompts for security from AI safety agents
not_for: Simple prompt storage (use a database or file storage for basic storage)
Production prompt injection protection (use dedicated AI safety tools)
Teams not doing prompt engineering or multi-agent development
best_when: An agent needs to manage a library of system prompts for AI applications — for prompt versioning, template management, or organized prompt retrieval in multi-agent systems.
avoid_when: You just need simple text storage for prompts — a JSON file or database is sufficient without MCP overhead.

auth.notes: SystemPrompt.io API key required. Register at systemprompt.io. API key passed as environment variable. May also support local-only mode.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SystemPrompt.io: free tier with limited prompt storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid tiers for more prompts and team features
pricing.notes: Freemium model. Free tier available. Community MCP server is open source.

known_agent_gotchas: SystemPrompt.io is a newer service — stability and long-term support unproven
API key tied to external service — prompt data stored in SystemPrompt.io cloud
System prompts stored externally — data sovereignty consideration for sensitive prompts
Community MCP by Ejb503 — may not be officially maintained by SystemPrompt.io
Prompt injection security: storing prompts externally creates supply chain risk
Free tier limits may be very restrictive for production use cases
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth. Prompts stored in cloud (SystemPrompt.io). Community MCP. Store API key securely. Consider: system prompts sent to external service — review for sensitive content.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key. Prompts in cloud — review data sovereignty. Community MCP. Store API key securely.
